message:
feat(raptorq): liquid glass navbar, editorial typography, and paragraph spacing

Three improvements to the RaptorQ article reading experience:

1. LIQUID GLASS NAVBAR (site-header.tsx)
   Enhanced the global site header with Apple-style frosted glass:
   - Backdrop blur: 12px → 24px for stronger content diffusion
   - Added saturate(1.8) vibrancy boost via spring-animated motion value
   - Background opacity: 0.8 → 0.55 (more translucent, lets content show)
   - Border: dark slate → rgba(255,255,255,0.12) subtle white glass edge
   - Combined blur+saturate into single useTransform([smoothBlur, smoothSaturate])
     for reactive updates on both axes simultaneously

2. EDITORIAL TYPOGRAPHY (globals.css)
   Applied the three Google Fonts that were loaded but never assigned:
   - Body text: Crimson Pro serif (was falling back to system font stack)
   - Display/section titles: Bricolage Grotesque geometric sans
   - Code elements: JetBrains Mono with ligatures
   - Desktop paragraph font: 1.1rem → 1.2rem, line-height 1.6 → 1.7
   - Mobile paragraph font: 1rem → 1.125rem, line-height → 1.75

3. PARAGRAPH SPACING — CSS SELECTOR BUG FIX (globals.css)
   Root cause: The CSS rule `.raptorq-scope .rq-body p` used a descendant
   combinator (space), but both classes live on the SAME <div> element.
   CSS descendant selectors require actual parent-child nesting — an
   element is not its own descendant. Since Tailwind v4 preflight sets
   `p { margin: 0 }`, paragraphs had zero spacing.
   Fix: Changed all 5 instances of `.raptorq-scope .rq-body` to the
   compound selector `.raptorq-scope.rq-body` (no space), which correctly
   matches both classes on one element. Paragraphs now get proper
   margin-bottom: 1.75em between them.

Includes .beads/ state for closed beads bd-3vil and bd-13xi.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
